speaker: interviewee
text: [INAUDIBLE]

speaker: interviewee
text: I don't know.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, this is tape seven. Albert, if you can introduce your wife and your daughter to us?

speaker: interviewee
text: My wife, Gertrude, [? or Trude. ?] [PAUSES] And my daughter, Anna-- oldest-- older daughter.

speaker: interviewer
text: Gertrude, can I ask you, how has Albert's experiences affected your life together?

speaker: interviewee
text: A little bit, because he had a hard time. Of course this has affected me a little bit. I have tried to do my best, yeah, to bring him to the reality. I hope I succeeded. I don't know. You'll have to ask him.

speaker: interviewer
text: Has she succeeded?

speaker: interviewee
text: Have I succeeded?

speaker: interviewee
text: I think yes.

speaker: interviewer
text: And do you want to say anything, Anya-- Anna?

speaker: interviewee
text: No, not really.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. Is there anything else you would like to say, Gertrude? Any message for, for anybody? Any message for us?

speaker: interviewee
text: What kind of message do you mean?

speaker: interviewer
text: Anything you want to say to your grandchildren, who unfortunately aren't here?

speaker: interviewee
text: [LAUGHS] My grandchildren, I hope they won't have this terrible experience what their grandfather had. And I hope they never will have this experience. They only can read it from the tape, what their grandfather had. I wish for them the best of luck.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. Thank you for sharing that with us.

speaker: interviewee
text: What?

speaker: interviewee
text: Comments.

speaker: interviewee
text: [? What is it? ?]

speaker: interviewee
text: End-- comments.

speaker: interviewee
text: The end.

speaker: interviewer
text: Yes? Albert, can you explain to me about your cap on your head? What-- what is this on your head?

speaker: interviewee
text: This? What?

speaker: interviewer
text: Yes.

speaker: interviewer
text: [INAUDIBLE]

speaker: interviewer
text: Shh, please. Thanks a lot.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is the Czechoslovakia emblem.

speaker: interviewer
text: Uh-huh. And did you wear that in the army?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes.

speaker: interviewer
text: Right.

speaker: interviewee
text: Wore on the uniform, you know?

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. Now can you tell me about your medals? Can you show me each medal and tell me what it is?

speaker: interviewee
text: This medal [POINTING] is the War Cross. [PAUSES] Yes?

speaker: interviewer
text: Yes. When did you get that?

speaker: interviewee
text: The Czechoslovak War Cross. I got it after Kiev.

speaker: interviewer
text: Right.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is the picture there. This second [PAUSES] is the-- the Red Star. [PAUSES] I got it after the war. Yes?

speaker: interviewer
text: These two [POINTS] and the third I lost was Orders. Yes? These are medals-- Wait a minute. The red one you got from the Czechoslovakian army, yes?

speaker: interviewee
text: All from the Czechoslovak army.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK.

speaker: interviewee
text: And this one is one from the army, that I was in the army-- is a medal.

speaker: interviewer
text: Mm-hmm.

speaker: interviewee
text: And the other one is from the finish the army.

speaker: interviewer
text: When you left?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes.

speaker: interviewer
text: And you got those for bravery and for your command.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes.

speaker: interviewer
text: Yes. And-- and for any other reason?

speaker: interviewee
text: Same in the war-- bravery, you know? It was difficult.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. Did you get them all? OK, thank you, Albert. [PAUSES] OK, Albert, who is this photograph of?

speaker: interviewee
text: It's my parents-- mother and father.

speaker: interviewer
text: Do you know how old they were then?

speaker: interviewee
text: They are born, the father, 1867. Mother was born 1874. They're over 120 now.

speaker: interviewer
text: And how old do you think they were in that photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: In this photograph, this maybe, [PAUSES] I don't know, 25, 30.

speaker: interviewer
text: So they were just married, do you think?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. It must be in the time they married, in 1890-something.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where-- where would they be living? Where would that photo be?

speaker: interviewee
text: This photo was somewhere in Galicia. I don't know the place.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, Albert, can you explain these two photographs?

speaker: interviewee
text: That's I am after the war in Kiev, in the Ukraine. And this is my late wife in the uniform.

speaker: interviewer
text: And how old would you have been in that photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: In this photograph, I must be [PAUSES] 28, 29.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what year would that be?

speaker: interviewee
text: What?

speaker: interviewer
text: What year?

speaker: interviewee
text: This?

speaker: interviewer
text: Yes.

speaker: interviewee
text: This was in 1943 or '44. I don't remember.

speaker: interviewer
text: And your wife?

speaker: interviewee
text: Same. It was in Buzuluk. She wasn't-- at this time on the front, she was in Buzuluk.

speaker: interviewer
text: And how old was she?

speaker: interviewee
text: She was around same age.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. OK, Albert, can you explain this photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was last year on the VE Day, VE Day. I wear the first time there-- medals.

speaker: interviewer
text: Why for the first time?

speaker: interviewee
text: Never before after the war.

speaker: interviewer
text: You never wanted to wear them before?

speaker: interviewee
text: No, no no! It wasn't [PAUSES] time or something to wear it.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. OK, can you explain this photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. It's the same photograph, now with my daughter Anna.

speaker: interviewer
text: What time of year is this?

speaker: interviewee
text: Last year.

speaker: interviewer
text: In the summer?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. [PAUSES] No, it was-- April--

speaker: interviewer
text: April or May.

speaker: interviewee
text: --beginning of May.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, can you explain this photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes, this is my sister.

speaker: interviewer
text: Which one is your sister?

speaker: interviewee
text: The younger. The Katerina.

speaker: interviewer
text: She's on the left or the right?

speaker: interviewee
text: On the left. And there's my younger daughter, Tanya.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where was this photo taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: Somewhere in London.

speaker: interviewer
text: And how old was your sister in this photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: In this photograph, she was over 80.

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you explain this photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is [? Trude, ?] my wife, and [? I am. ?]

speaker: interviewer
text: And where are you?

speaker: interviewee
text: I think it was in Marienbad, no?

speaker: interviewer
text: When, when was it taken? Last year?

speaker: interviewee
text: Last year.

speaker: interviewer
text: Yes? This photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: Same in the Czech Republic, last year.

speaker: interviewer
text: That's 1995 or 19--

speaker: interviewer
text: 1996? Which year?

speaker: interviewee
text: No, this year!

speaker: interviewer
text: Oh, this year?

speaker: interviewee
text: Sorry. '96. This is the-- my younger daughter, Tanya.

speaker: interviewer
text: Right. And you took her there for a holiday, or you went there, [PAUSES] to where this photo is taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: In Marienbad.

speaker: interviewer
text: Yes, you went for a holiday?

speaker: interviewee
text: For a holiday, yes.

speaker: interviewer
text: With your family?

speaker: interviewee
text: No, with my wife, and they comes to see us.

speaker: interviewer
text: All right. OK, Albert, can you explain this photograph?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my granddaughter Gabby. She's a member of the British Gymnasts' Squad.

speaker: interviewer
text: And how old is she?

speaker: interviewee
text: She will be-- no, she will be 18 in January.

speaker: interviewer
text: And when was this photograph taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: I think last year or something.

speaker: interviewer
text: And she's your granddaughter who lives in London?

speaker: interviewee
text: In London-- Harrow.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, can you explain this photograph, Albert?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. [PAUSES] The first on the left, I am; the grand, granddaughter, Katarine; the grandson--

speaker: interviewer
text: There's another girl.

speaker: interviewee
text: Other girl?

speaker: interviewer
text: Mhm.

speaker: interviewee
text: Camilla. No! Gabby. Granddaughter Gabby. [PAUSES] This is the grandson Mark; [PAUSES] the oldest grandson, Nicholas; [PAUSES] and the granddaughter Camilla.

speaker: interviewer
text: And when was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: I think early this year, some--

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you. Can you explain this to me, Albert?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. This is a rehabilitation from '92, yes, that I was rehabilitated. They were Czechoslovak citizen, yes? And give me back my military ranks, yes? And I got back my colonel rank.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where does it say that?

speaker: interviewee
text: What?

speaker: interviewer
text: Where does it say it? What are the words?

speaker: interviewee
text: There. I can't see so far.

speaker: interviewer
text: Underneath the alphabetical list?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes.

speaker: interviewer
text: Is that right?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes.

speaker: interviewer
text: Rehabilitation, the first two words--

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes.

speaker: interviewer
text: --are the colonel?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you.

speaker: interviewee
text: It's a invitation from the British government to VE Day in May.

speaker: interviewer
text: Why have they spelt your name with two Ss?

speaker: interviewee
text: Somebody wrote it with two Ss. Somebody was German [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. And what is this, Albert?

speaker: interviewee
text: It's the invitation from the Queen to St. Paul's Cathedral-- the VE Day, the 5th of May, I think.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. And what is this, Albert?

speaker: interviewee
text: It is some program for the VE Day.

speaker: interviewer
text: And who has signed it? Or have you signed it?

speaker: interviewee
text: It's signed the general from the Czech embassy.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what is his name?

speaker: interviewee
text: I don't know, to be honest.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. [PAUSES] What does it say there in Czech? What does that mean?

speaker: interviewee
text: Only the best of wishes [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. Thank you.
